source: Invest Like the Best (YouTube) — Watts and wafers
summary: TerraFab (SpaceX/Tesla joint venture with Intel partnership) will succeed in building a leading US semiconductor fab, potentially disrupting the TSMC/Intel/Samsung dynamic
summary_extended: Baker argues that TerraFab — a reported SpaceX/Tesla joint venture partnered with Intel — has a credible path to becoming a leading US semiconductor fab, potentially disrupting the current TSMC/Samsung/Intel hierarchy. His core reasoning draws on a structural analogy: just as TSMC rose to dominance partly because wafer fab equipment companies (ASML, KLA, Lam, Applied Materials) prioritized it with their best engineering teams, TerraFab will receive similar A-team support because those same equipment vendors want to avoid a TSMC monopsony and will back a credible challenger. Baker adds that Elon Musk's global reputation will attract elite semiconductor engineering talent, and that he will build cultural infrastructure — Taiwan towns, Japan towns, Korea towns — to recruit diaspora engineers who built TSMC and Samsung. Baker believes Elon will compress traditional fab development timelines significantly, making TerraFab a long-term structural threat to incumbent fabs and a long-term beneficiary thesis for the semi-cap equipment suppliers who will be deeply embedded in its buildout.
detail: Baker is bullish on TerraFab succeeding because: (1) Intel partnership provides 50 years of institutional knowledge; (2) semi-cap equipment A-teams (ASML, KLA, Lam, Applied Materials) will prioritize it because they don't want a monopsony; (3) Elon's reputation will attract the best global engineering talent; (4) Elon will build cultural infrastructure (Taiwan town, Japan town, Korea town) to recruit top engineers. He believes Elon will move faster than traditional fab timelines.
confidence_evidence: Baker makes specific structural arguments for why TerraFab will succeed and draws on his knowledge of how TSMC caught Intel (semi-cap equipment company support). He frames Elon's approach as fundamentally different from how Intel/Samsung think.
quote_fragment: A teams at the wafer fab equipment companies will be here

ticker_audit.reasoning: Intel is named as a direct partner in TerraFab, contributing 50 years of institutional fab knowledge; the thesis is partly about Intel's role and how this partnership changes Intel's strategic position.

Tesla is cited as a co-founder of TerraFab but the thesis is about TerraFab the fab entity and the semi-cap equipment ecosystem, not about Tesla's business or financials. Tesla equity is not a direct subject or beneficiary of a semiconductor fab thesis in any unambiguous way.

Baker explicitly argues that ASML and peer semi-cap equipment companies will prioritize TerraFab with their A-teams, making them direct and early embedded beneficiaries of TerraFab's buildout — mirroring how they benefited from TSMC's rise.
KLA is one of the semi-cap equipment companies Baker explicitly names as likely to send A-teams to TerraFab, making it a direct beneficiary of the fab's construction and ramp.
Lam Research is explicitly named among the wafer fab equipment companies Baker expects to prioritize TerraFab, making it a direct beneficiary.
Applied Materials is explicitly named among the semi-cap equipment companies Baker expects to support TerraFab with their best teams, making it a direct beneficiary.
